behavior_summary: Presents the full BRIBOOK text to the engine's book-reader routine so a player/actor can read the book.
Initializes some item/display metadata (via a 'set info' opcode) before creating the temporary string containing the book text.
Creates a large literal string (the book content), converts it to a pointer, calls Book::read with that pointer and a caller-supplied buffer/context, then frees the temporary string.
Propagates the return value from Book::read as this routine's return value, executes a suspend, and then returns.
notable_control_flow: Executes a 'set info' opcode prior to pushing the book text (initialization of display/item metadata).
Allocates or materializes a large literal string for the book, converts it to a pointer and passes it to Book::read.
Calls Book::read and captures its return value, then frees the temporary string buffer.
Performs a suspend before returning the Book::read result to the caller.
open_questions: Which exact info field is being set by the 'set info' opcode and how does it affect book presentation or item state?
What is the precise meaning/structure of the caller-supplied buffer_ptr passed at BP+06h (UI buffer, book object, or caller context)?
Are there any side effects of Book::read beyond display (e.g., logging progress, advancing quests) that this script relies on?
Is the large literal stored in script data or allocated at runtime, and are there edge cases in memory management for very long texts?

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK::0080
main_function.purpose: Prepare and supply the BRIBOOK narrative text to the engine's book-reading routine so the player/actor can read the book; manage temporary string lifecycle and return the reader's result.
entry_conditions: Triggered when the in-game book item is activated/used (player or actor opens/reads the book).
Inferred: invoked by an item use/read event mapped to the BRIBOOK script.

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK::0080
function_map.role: Provide book text, initialize display metadata, call Book::read, and manage the temporary string lifecycle.

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: Engine routine to render/process/display the provided book text to the player; returns a status/result propagated by BRIBOOK::0080.

external_calls.purpose: Render/process/display the supplied book text to the player; receives a pointer to the temporary string and a caller-supplied buffer/context pointer and returns a result propagated by the script.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Presents the full BRIBOOK text to the engine's book-reader routine so a player/actor can read the book.
